Title: Self-register a new portal account with valid identity and email verification
Preconditions: Portal self-registration enabled; SMTP configured; no existing account for the email
Test_Steps: 1. Open portal registration page | 2. Enter demographics and unique email | 3. Set a compliant password | 4. Submit registration | 5. Open verification email and click activation link | 6. Log in with new credentials
Expected_Result: Account is created in pending state, verification email is sent, activation link confirms the account, and login succeeds after activation

Title: Reject self-registration with an email already linked to an existing account
Preconditions: An active portal account already exists for the target email
Test_Steps: 1. Open registration page | 2. Enter demographics with the existing email | 3. Set a valid password | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: Registration is rejected with a non-enumerating message; no duplicate account is created and no detail about the existing account is leaked

Title: Password policy boundary checks during self-registration
Preconditions: Self-registration enabled; password policy = min 8, upper, lower, digit, special
Test_Steps: 1. Enter password of length 7 | 2. Submit and observe | 3. Enter password of length 8 meeting all classes | 4. Submit | 5. Enter 128-char password | 6. Submit | 7. Enter 129-char password | 8. Submit
Test_Data: Pw7=Ab1!def; Pw8=Ab1!defg; Pw128=<128 valid chars>; Pw129=<129 valid chars>
Expected_Result: 7-char and 129-char are rejected at boundary; 8-char and 128-char are accepted; policy violations show field-level messages

Title: Identity matching of self-registrant to an existing patient record (decision table)
Preconditions: Existing patient records present; demographic matching service enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Submit self-registration with varying match attributes | 2. Observe match decision (auto-link, manual review, new record) | 3. Verify resulting linkage
Test_Data: Combos: Name+DOB+MRN match -> auto-link; Name+DOB match, MRN blank -> manual review; Name only -> new record; conflicting DOB -> manual review
Expected_Result: Each input combination yields the correct linkage outcome per the decision table; ambiguous matches route to staff review, never silent auto-link

Title: Reject self-registration of a minor below the self-access age threshold
Preconditions: Minimum self-registration age configured (e.g., 13/18 per jurisdiction)
Test_Steps: 1. Enter DOB making patient below threshold | 2. Complete remaining fields | 3. Submit
Test_Data: DOB=2015-01-01 (age below threshold); jurisdiction min age=18
Expected_Result: Self-registration is blocked with guidance to use proxy/guardian access; no patient-facing self account is provisioned

Title: Portal login lockout after consecutive failed attempts
Preconditions: Account exists; lockout threshold=5 failed attempts; lockout window configured
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt login with wrong password 5 times | 2. Attempt 6th login with correct password | 3. Wait lockout window or use unlock flow | 4. Login again
Expected_Result: Account locks after 5 failures; correct password during lockout still fails; account unlocks only after window/unlock; lockout is audit-logged

Title: Enforce multi-factor authentication on portal login
Preconditions: MFA enrolled (TOTP) for the portal account
Test_Steps: 1. Enter valid username and password | 2. Submit | 3. Enter invalid OTP | 4. Enter valid OTP | 5. Complete login
Expected_Result: Password alone does not grant access; invalid OTP is rejected; valid OTP completes login; failed OTP attempts are rate-limited and logged

Title: Portal session timeout and re-authentication for PHI access
Preconditions: Logged-in portal session; idle timeout configured (e.g., 15 min)
Test_Steps: 1. Log in and view a record | 2. Leave session idle past timeout | 3. Attempt to view PHI | 4. Re-authenticate | 5. Resume
Expected_Result: Idle session expires; PHI access after expiry forces re-authentication; no PHI is rendered from a stale session token

Title: Password reset via emailed token with single-use and expiry
Preconditions: Portal account exists; SMTP configured; reset token TTL configured
Test_Steps: 1. Request password reset | 2. Receive token email | 3. Use token to set new password | 4. Reuse the same token | 5. Request again, let token expire, then use it
Expected_Result: Token states transition Issued -> Consumed -> Invalid; reused token is rejected; expired token is rejected; successful reset invalidates old sessions

Title: Patient views own visit and encounter history
Preconditions: Patient account linked to a record with prior visits/encounters
Test_Steps: 1. Log in to portal | 2. Open Health Records | 3. Open Visits/Encounters | 4. Open an encounter detail
Expected_Result: Patient sees own encounters with date, type, provider; data matches clinical source of record; no other patients' data is shown

Title: Lab result release hold (embargo) before clinician review
Preconditions: Result release policy with embargo for sensitive/abnormal results configured
Test_Steps: 1. Generate normal, abnormal, and sensitive results | 2. Apply release rules | 3. View patient portal results list
Test_Data: Normal -> immediate; Abnormal -> embargo until review; Sensitive(HIV/genetic) -> manual release
Expected_Result: Each result type follows its release rule; embargoed results are hidden until released; released-at timestamp and releaser are audited

Title: Display result reference ranges and abnormal flags correctly
Preconditions: Released numeric lab results with reference ranges available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Open Results | 3. Open a result with low, normal, and high analytes
Expected_Result: Each analyte shows value, units, reference range, and correct H/N/L flag matching the source; no rounding/unit errors

Title: Book an appointment in an available slot
Preconditions: Appointment scheduling enabled; bookable provider/service with open slots
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Open Appointments | 3. Select service and provider | 4. Pick an open slot | 5. Confirm booking
Expected_Result: Appointment is created and visible to patient and clinic schedule; confirmation is shown; slot is no longer offered to others

Title: Prevent double-booking of the same slot under concurrent requests
Preconditions: Single open slot; two portal sessions attempting the same slot
Test_Steps: 1. Open the same slot in two sessions | 2. Confirm booking in session A | 3. Confirm booking in session B near-simultaneously
Expected_Result: Only one booking succeeds; the second receives a slot-unavailable error; no overbooking and no orphaned hold remains

Title: Booking boundary at clinic lead-time and scheduling horizon
Preconditions: Min lead time (e.g., 2h) and max horizon (e.g., 90 days) configured
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt booking 1 min before min lead time | 2. Attempt exactly at min lead time | 3. Attempt at max horizon | 4. Attempt 1 day beyond horizon
Expected_Result: Bookings inside lead time and beyond horizon are rejected; exactly-at-boundary bookings are accepted

Title: Cancel an upcoming appointment within the cancellation window
Preconditions: Patient has a future booked appointment; cancellation window configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Appointments | 2. Select the upcoming appointment | 3. Cancel | 4. Confirm cancellation | 5. Re-open the appointment
Expected_Result: Appointment transitions Booked -> Cancelled; slot is released back to availability; status and reason are recorded and audited

Title: Block self-cancellation after the cancellation cutoff
Preconditions: Appointment within the no-self-cancel cutoff window
Test_Steps: 1. Open an appointment inside cutoff | 2. Attempt to cancel
Expected_Result: Self-cancellation is blocked with guidance to contact the clinic; appointment remains Booked; attempt is logged

Title: Appointment lifecycle state transitions are valid only along allowed paths
Preconditions: Appointment workflow with states Requested/Booked/CheckedIn/Completed/Cancelled/NoShow
Test_Steps: 1. Drive appointment through each transition | 2. Attempt illegal transitions (e.g., Completed -> Booked) | 3. Verify final states
Expected_Result: Only allowed transitions succeed; illegal transitions are rejected; terminal states cannot revert; each change is timestamped and audited

Title: Patient sends a secure message to the care team
Preconditions: Secure messaging enabled; patient linked to a care team
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Open Messages | 3. Compose to care team | 4. Add subject and body | 5. Send
Test_Data: To=Care Team; Subject=Medication question; Body=Refill needed
Expected_Result: Message is delivered to the care team inbox, appears in patient sent items, is stored encrypted, and is timestamped

Title: Reject message attachments exceeding size/type policy
Preconditions: Messaging attachment policy: max 10MB; allowlist pdf/jpg/png
Test_Steps: 1. Compose message | 2. Attach 11MB file | 3. Attach .exe file | 4. Attach valid 2MB pdf | 5. Send
Expected_Result: Oversized and disallowed-type attachments are rejected with clear errors; valid attachment is accepted; no executable is stored

Title: Sanitize message content against stored XSS injection
Preconditions: Secure messaging enabled; staff inbox renders patient messages
Test_Steps: 1. Compose message with script payload in body and subject | 2. Send | 3. Open message in patient thread | 4. Open message in staff inbox
Expected_Result: Payload is neutralized/encoded on render in both patient and staff views; no script executes; stored value is safely escaped

Title: Capture and version patient consent for portal data sharing
Preconditions: Consent module enabled; current consent policy version published
Test_Steps: 1. Log in first time | 2. Present consent form | 3. Accept consent | 4. Publish new policy version | 5. Log in again
Expected_Result: Consent is captured with policy version, timestamp, and signer; on new version, re-consent is required before continued access; both versions retained

Title: Withdraw consent and enforce downstream access restriction
Preconditions: Patient previously granted consent for data sharing/messaging
Test_Steps: 1. Open Consent settings | 2. Withdraw consent | 3. Confirm | 4. Attempt the consented action (e.g., share/download) | 5. Verify audit
Expected_Result: Consent transitions Granted -> Withdrawn; dependent actions are blocked after withdrawal; withdrawal time/reason are audited; no retroactive data leak

Title: Granular consent scopes map correctly to FHIR Consent provisions
Preconditions: FHIR Consent resource supported; scopes for messaging, sharing, research
Test_Steps: 1. Set granular scopes in portal | 2. Retrieve FHIR Consent resource | 3. Validate provision.type and provision.action mapping
Expected_Result: FHIR Consent resource reflects each scope as permit/deny provisions with correct category and period; round-trips without data loss

Title: Patient exports own record via FHIR Patient/$everything
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; patient authorized via SMART scopes for own data
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate with patient-scoped token | 2. Call GET [base]/Patient/{id}/$everything | 3. Inspect returned Bundle
Expected_Result: Returns a searchset/collection Bundle containing only the requesting patient's resources; references resolve; no other patient data present

Title: Reject $everything access to another patient's record
Preconditions: Patient-scoped SMART token bound to patient A
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as patient A | 2. Call Patient/{B}/$everything for a different patient B
Expected_Result: Request is denied with 403/forbidden (or empty per policy); no cross-patient PHI is returned; denial is audit-logged

Title: Bulk Data export kickoff returns 202 with Content-Location polling
Preconditions: FHIR Bulk Data ($export) enabled; backend authorization (SMART backend services)
Test_Steps: 1. POST [base]/$export with Prefer: respond-async | 2. Capture Content-Location | 3. Poll status endpoint until complete | 4. Download NDJSON files
Expected_Result: Kickoff returns 202 with Content-Location; polling returns in-progress then 200 with output file manifest; files are valid NDJSON per resource type

Title: Bulk export status and error handling (decision/state)
Preconditions: Bulk export job initiated
Test_Steps: 1. Kickoff async export | 2. Poll in-progress | 3. Cancel via DELETE on Content-Location | 4. Kickoff again and force error | 5. Poll
Expected_Result: States transition Accepted -> InProgress -> Completed/Cancelled/Error; DELETE cancels job (202); errors return OperationOutcome; Retry-After honored

Title: Download human-readable record summary (PDF/CCD)
Preconditions: Document export feature enabled; patient has clinical data
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Open Download My Record | 3. Choose summary format | 4. Generate and download
Expected_Result: File downloads with patient's clinical summary; content matches portal data; PHI watermark/header present; download is audited

Title: Guardian proxy access to a minor's record within age policy
Preconditions: Proxy relationship established; minor under proxy-eligible age
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as guardian | 2. Switch to minor's profile | 3. View permitted records
Expected_Result: Guardian sees only permitted minor data scoped by policy; context switch is explicit and audited; guardian's own data is separate

Title: Adolescent confidentiality redaction at age-of-majority transition
Preconditions: Adolescent confidentiality rules configured; sensitive categories defined
Test_Steps: 1. Set minor ages around adolescent threshold | 2. Guardian views records | 3. Check confidential categories (reproductive, mental health, substance) | 4. Cross threshold
Expected_Result: Below threshold full access; in adolescent band confidential categories are redacted from guardian; at majority guardian proxy auto-terminates per policy

Title: Auto-revoke guardian proxy when minor reaches majority
Preconditions: Active guardian proxy on a minor approaching majority age
Test_Steps: 1. Advance minor to majority age (test clock) | 2. Guardian attempts to view record | 3. Verify proxy status
Test_Data: Minor DOB making majority on test date
Expected_Result: Proxy transitions Active -> Expired at majority; guardian access is blocked; patient self-access path is enabled; transition is audited

Title: Revoke a previously granted proxy and verify access denial
Preconditions: Active proxy relationship between proxy P and patient X
Test_Steps: 1. Admin/patient revokes proxy | 2. Proxy P logs in | 3. Proxy attempts to open patient X profile
Expected_Result: Revoked proxy can no longer view patient X data; attempt returns access denied and is audited; no cached PHI is served

Title: Pairwise coverage of role x consent x data-category access decisions
Preconditions: RBAC, consent scopes, and data sensitivity categories configured
Test_Steps: 1. Generate pairwise combos of {role: self,guardian,revoked-proxy} x {consent: granted,withdrawn} x {category: general,sensitive} | 2. Attempt access for each | 3. Record allow/deny
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination yields the policy-correct allow/deny; no combination grants access beyond policy; denials are audited

Title: Patient updates own demographics/contact with verification
Preconditions: Logged-in patient; demographics self-edit enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open Profile | 2. Edit phone and address | 3. Verify new phone via OTP | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Changes save after verification; record updates propagate to source; change history is retained; unverified phone is not saved

Title: Validate demographic field formats and boundaries
Preconditions: Profile edit enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Enter invalid email format | 2. Enter future DOB | 3. Enter max-length name (255) and 256 chars | 4. Enter non-numeric phone | 5. Save each
Expected_Result: Invalid email, future DOB, over-max name, and non-numeric phone are rejected; valid 255-char value accepted; messages are field-specific

Title: Request a medication refill from active medication list
Preconditions: Patient has an active prescription eligible for refill
Test_Steps: 1. Open Medications | 2. Select an eligible medication | 3. Request refill | 4. Submit to pharmacy/provider
Expected_Result: Refill request is routed to provider/pharmacy queue; status shows Requested; patient sees confirmation; request is audited

Title: Block refill request for controlled or zero-refill medication
Preconditions: Patient has a controlled substance and a zero-refill medication
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt refill on controlled medication | 2. Attempt refill on zero-refill medication
Expected_Result: Refill is blocked with policy message directing to provider visit; no refill request is created; attempt is logged

Title: Keyboard-only navigation and visible focus across portal flows
Preconditions: Portal UI available; WCAG 2.1 AA target
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate login, dashboard, appointments via keyboard only | 2. Tab through all interactive elements | 3. Operate controls with Enter/Space | 4. Observe focus order and visibility
Expected_Result: All interactive elements are reachable and operable by keyboard; focus is visible and logical; no keyboard traps

Title: Screen reader announces result tables and form errors
Preconditions: Screen reader (NVDA/VoiceOver); released results and a form with validation
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate results table with screen reader | 2. Trigger form validation errors | 3. Listen to announcements | 4. Check labels and roles
Expected_Result: Table headers/cells are correctly associated and announced; errors are programmatically associated and announced; all inputs have labels

Title: Color contrast and text resize compliance for clinical data
Preconditions: Portal pages with abnormal-flag color coding
Test_Steps: 1. Measure contrast of text, links, abnormal flags | 2. Zoom to 200% | 3. Verify reflow and no loss of content
Expected_Result: Contrast meets AA; abnormal flags are not conveyed by color alone; at 200% content reflows without loss or horizontal scroll

Title: Audit log captures portal PHI access and disclosures
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; DB/audit store accessible to QA
Test_Steps: 1. Perform view-record, download, message, and proxy-switch | 2. Query audit store | 3. Validate entries
Expected_Result: Each PHI access/disclosure creates an immutable audit entry with who/what/when/where and outcome; no gaps; entries are queryable and tamper-evident

Title: IDOR protection on record and appointment object references
Preconditions: Two patient accounts with known record/appointment IDs
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as patient A | 2. Request patient B's record id via direct URL/API | 3. Request patient B's appointment id | 4. Inspect responses
Expected_Result: All cross-account object references are denied (403/404 per policy); no PHI leakage; attempts are audited

Title: Appointment reminder and result-ready notification delivery and opt-out
Preconditions: Notification channels (email/SMS) configured; patient opted in
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger appointment reminder | 2. Trigger result-ready notification | 3. Opt out of SMS | 4. Re-trigger
Expected_Result: Reminders and result-ready alerts deliver via opted-in channels without PHI in clear text; opt-out suppresses that channel; preferences are honored and audited

Title: FHIR export reflects record corrections/amendments accurately
Preconditions: A clinical value was corrected; FHIR export available
Test_Steps: 1. Correct an observation in source | 2. Run Patient/$everything | 3. Inspect the observation status/history
Expected_Result: Exported resource reflects the amended value with status=amended/corrected; superseded value is not presented as current; history is consistent

Title: Inbound HL7 ADT updates reflect in patient portal demographics
Preconditions: HL7 v2 ADT interface feeding the patient index
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 update for the patient | 2. Process message | 3. View demographics in portal
Expected_Result: Portal demographics reflect the ADT update after processing; mismatched/invalid PID is rejected to error queue; change is audited

Title: Exploratory testing of self-registration abuse and edge inputs
Preconditions: Self-registration enabled in a test environment
Test_Steps: 1. Probe with unicode/emoji names, very long inputs, RTL text, leading/trailing spaces | 2. Rapid repeated submissions | 3. Disposable email domains | 4. Browser back/refresh mid-flow
Expected_Result: No crashes or data corruption; inputs are normalized/validated; rate limiting curbs abuse; partial submissions do not create ghost accounts; findings logged

Title: Patient adds a result comment/question routed to care team
Preconditions: Released result with patient-annotation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open a released result | 2. Add a question/comment | 3. Submit | 4. Verify routing
Expected_Result: Comment is saved against the result, routed to the care team as a message, visible to patient, and audited; does not alter the clinical value

Title: Pairwise login matrix across browser, channel and credential validity
Preconditions: Portal accessible on multiple browsers/devices; MFA optional flag
Test_Steps: 1. Build pairwise combos of {browser: Chrome,Firefox,Safari} x {device: desktop,mobile} x {creds: valid,invalid} x {MFA: on,off} | 2. Attempt login per combo | 3. Record outcome
Expected_Result: Valid+correct-MFA combos succeed on all browser/device pairs; invalid creds or wrong MFA fail consistently; no environment-specific bypass

Title: Bulk export NDJSON conforms to requested _type and US Core profiles
Preconditions: Completed bulk export output files available
Test_Steps: 1. Download NDJSON output files | 2. Parse each line as a FHIR resource | 3. Validate resourceType matches file _type | 4. Validate against profile
Expected_Result: Each NDJSON line is a valid resource of the declared type; required US Core elements present; counts match manifest; no malformed lines
